sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Is Abbott (ABT) Insider Buying Offsetting Concerns Around Nutrition and Diagnostics Earnings Pressure?
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories reported a Q4 2025 earnings shortfall, particularly in its Nutrition and Diagnostics segments, leading to a dip in net income and diluted EPS compared to the previous year. Despite a recent 7% share price slump and cautious analyst revisions, CEO Robert Ford made a significant insider purchase of over US$2 million in Abbott shares. This move signals leadership's confidence in the company's long-term outlook, aiming to counter concerns arising from the earnings pressure and heightened execution risk in key segments.

feed.title: Indonesian stocks tumble 7% after MSCI applies interim freeze on investability risks
feed.summary: Indonesian stocks experienced a significant 7% drop after MSCI implemented an interim freeze on the country's investability. This decision was prompted by concerns regarding market accessibility and investment risks. The article highlights the immediate negative impact of MSCI's action on the Indonesian stock market.

feed.title: Finward Bancorp earnings missed by $0.15, revenue fell short of estimates
feed.summary: Finward Bancorp reported fourth-quarter EPS of $0.46, missing analyst estimates by $0.15, and revenue of $16.5M, falling short of the consensus estimate of $18.25M. Despite the earnings miss, the company's stock has performed well, rising 4.24% in the last three months and 37.74% in the last 12 months.

feed.title: CA$61.5M Denison Partnership Positions Explorer for High-Grade Uranium Discovery Potential
feed.summary: Skyharbour Resources Ltd. is embarking on a significant exploration initiative in 2026, including over 15,000 meters of diamond drilling across newly formed Russell Lake joint ventures with Denison Mines Corp. This partnership, valued at up to CA$61.5 million, aims to accelerate high-grade uranium discoveries in the Athabasca Basin, capitalizing on rising uranium prices and increased demand driven by AI and nuclear power expansion. Skyharbour anticipates extensive news flow throughout 2026 from its various projects.

feed.title: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) Settles SEC Charges with $40M Penal
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) has agreed to pay a $40 million civil penalty to settle SEC charges of inflating the performance of its nutrition segment. Three former executives, Vince Macciocchi, Ray Young, and Vikram Luthar, were also implicated, with Macciocchi and Young facing individual penalties. Despite strong revenue, the company's financial health shows mixed signals with lower profitability, and its valuation metrics suggest the stock is approaching overbought territory.

feed.title: Key facts: Goldman Sachs raises gold and aluminum price forecasts; aids Ellington Financial
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has raised its gold price target to $5,400 for December 2026, with a potential peak of $6,000 by spring 2026, driven by a shift away from dollar assets and increased retail interest. The bank also increased its 2026 aluminum price forecast to $3,150 per metric ton, despite revising the market surplus due to lower demand. Additionally, Goldman Sachs assisted Ellington Financial in raising approximately $118.5 million through a share sale to redeem preferred stock and for other uses.

feed.title: Con Edison Looks to Clean Energy to Address Future NYC Grid Reliability
feed.summary: Con Edison has issued a "Request for Information" to find clean, non-emitting solutions to address a projected 125-megawatt reliability gap in New York City's grid by summer 2032, potentially growing to 750 megawatts by 2036. The company is seeking proposals from various entities for strategies like energy efficiency, load shifting, battery storage, and distributed solar. These efforts are part of Con Edison's long-term planning to maintain its world-class reliability while complying with the Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act.

feed.title: Oracle Data Center Power Outage Disrupts TikTok For Users In US Days After App's High-Stakes Joint Venture Deal
feed.summary: A weather-related power outage at an Oracle data center caused disruptions for TikTok users in the U.S., impacting services days after the app's U.S. operations were restructured under new ownership. The outage led to slower load times and posting problems for users and occurred shortly after ByteDance finalized a joint venture deal to operate TikTok in the U.S. coinciding with a surge in app deletions due to user concern over updated privacy policies.

feed.title: Constellation Energy stock closes higher as PJM data-center rule overhaul keeps power prices in focus
feed.summary: Constellation Energy (CEG) stock closed up 1.2% at $288.76, driven by PJM Interconnection's proposed fast-track process for data centers and a "backstop" auction for new power capacity. These policy changes, particularly favoring existing power plants like nuclear, keep power market rules and wholesale power prices in focus. The company's stock performance also benefited from a broader uptick in power names and anticipates its next earnings update around February 17th.

feed.title: Cozy up for Valentine's Day at Beau Rivage
feed.summary: Beau Rivage Resort & Casino is offering various specials for Valentine's Day, including special dining menus at its restaurants like BR Prime, Coraline’s, Stalla, Jia, and Salt & Ivy. Guests can also enjoy spa and salon services, as well as shopping at The Jewelry Box and other retail stores for gifts. Gift cards are also available for those who prefer to give a flexible Valentine's treat.

feed.title: Why Lockheed Martin’s F-35 Still Has Shortfalls Despite A Record Year Of Deliveries
feed.summary: In 2025, Lockheed Martin's F-35 Lightning II program achieved a record 191 deliveries, marking a significant production milestone despite its history of delays and scrutiny. However, behind these impressive figures, the F-35 continues to face persistent operational challenges, including low mission-capable rates and complex sustainment issues, which affect its availability among global operators. The article explores how the F-35 remains a popular choice for many nations due to its advanced capabilities, yet its inherent complexity and a centralized logistics system hinder its full operational potential, necessitating continued investment in infrastructure and software stability.

feed.title: Driven Brands Completes IMO Sale to Refocus Portfolio
feed.summary: Driven Brands Holdings has completed the sale of its international car wash unit, IMO, for approximately €411 million, as part of a strategic move to refocus its portfolio. This divestiture aims to strengthen the balance sheet by primarily reducing debt and will lead to a new segment reporting structure, with Auto Glass Now becoming a stand-alone segment. Analysts currently rate DRVN stock as a Buy, while TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, assigns a Neutral score due to financial performance concerns, despite positive outlooks for the Take 5 business.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) Receives a Buy from D.A. Davidson
feed.summary: D.A. Davidson analyst Dan Waggoner maintained a Buy rating on Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) with a price target of $58.00. This follows a Buy rating from Morgan Stanley and a Hold rating from Citi, issued around the same time. The company reported a quarterly revenue of $3.3 billion and a net profit of $649 million in its latest earnings report, though corporate insider sentiment toward the stock is negative due to increased selling activity.

feed.title: News Corp Activates US$1 Billion Share Repurchase Program for Nasdaq-Listed Stock
feed.summary: News Corp has announced the activation of a share repurchase program, authorizing the buy-back of up to US$1 billion of its Nasdaq-listed Class A and Class B common stock. This initiative, part of its capital management strategy, aims to enhance earnings per share and improve capital structure efficiency. The repurchases will occur over time in the open market, subject to various market conditions.

feed.title: Waste Management: Growth Inflection Ahead, Volume Headwinds Fade And Pricing Stays Strong
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) is rated Buy, with attractive risk-reward as headwinds subside and valuation remains below historical and peer levels. The company's organic growth is expected to accelerate in 2026 due to industrial recovery, easing residential challenges, healthcare stabilization, and increasing renewable natural gas production. Margins should expand through strong pricing, automation, improved business mix, and normalization in healthcare, contributing to double-digit EPS growth in FY26 and FY27.

feed.title: Micron Starts Construction of New Advanced Fab in Singapore
feed.summary: Micron Technology has broken ground on a new advanced wafer fabrication facility in Singapore, investing $24 billion over the next decade. The facility, which will deliver 700,000 square feet of cleanroom space, is expected to begin initial wafer output in the second half of 2028 to meet rising demand for NAND flash driven by AI. This expansion will create approximately 1,600 jobs in engineering and operations and reinforces Singapore's role in the global semiconductor supply chain and advanced manufacturing.

feed.title: How a US$1 Billion Exclusive Contract At AdaptHealth (AHCO) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: AdaptHealth (AHCO) has secured a five-year, US$1 billion exclusive contract to provide home medical equipment and supplies to a large national healthcare system. This agreement is expected to generate at least US$200 million in annual revenue, strengthening the company's financial position and profitability. While this contract addresses previous concerns about trimmed guidance and supports AdaptHealth's investment narrative, execution risk remains a factor, especially given uncovered interest expenses.

feed.title: Dycom Establishes New $800 Million Term Loan Facility
feed.summary: Dycom Industries, Inc. has established a new $800 million Term Loan B facility, an amendment to its existing credit arrangements. The proceeds from this facility will be used to refinance a previous $600 million bridge loan, cover related expenses, and enhance the company's liquidity and capital structure. The Term Loan B features defined interest terms and quarterly amortization payments set to begin in September 2026.

feed.title: John Hancock financial opportunities fund director buys $10,001 in BTO
feed.summary: Kenneth J Phelan, a director of the John Hancock Financial Opportunities Fund (NYSE:BTO), recently purchased 269.569 common shares for a total of $10,001. The fund boasts a 7.03% dividend yield and strong financial health according to InvestingPro. Additionally, John Hancock has renewed share repurchase plans for six of its closed-end funds, including BTO, allowing them to buy back up to 10% of outstanding common shares.

feed.title: Intel says it will match government's 'Trump Accounts' contribution to kids of employees
feed.summary: Intel announced it will match the Trump administration's $1,000 payout for children of eligible U.S. employees as part of the "Trump Accounts" program, a move that signals a strengthening relationship between the chipmaker and the government. This program, designed to foster wealth-building for children, was initiated after the government acquired a 10% stake in Intel last year. Several other major companies are also participating in matching the federal contribution.

feed.title: KLA stock jumps nearly 5% as analysts lift targets ahead of Jan. 29 earnings
feed.summary: Shares of KLA (KLAC) rose nearly 5% ahead of its fiscal second-quarter earnings report, scheduled for January 29. Analysts at Evercore ISI and Wolfe Research raised their price targets for the chip-equipment maker, citing strong demand driven by AI infrastructure. Investors are closely watching KLA's guidance, particularly regarding AI-driven chip spending and potential impacts from U.S.-China trade tensions.

feed.title: Archer-Daniels-Midland, former executives charged by US SEC with accounting fraud
feed.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has filed settled charges against Archer-Daniels-Midland Co. and former executives Vince Macciocchi and Ray Young. The charges relate to allegations of materially inflating the performance of the company's Nutrition business segment. Additionally, the SEC has initiated a litigated action against former ADM executive Vikram Luthar regarding similar accounting fraud claims.

feed.title: Copart, Inc. (CPRT) Stock Falls Amid Market Uptick: What Investors Need to Know
feed.summary: Copart, Inc. (CPRT) saw its stock fall by 2.82% while the broader market experienced an uptick. Investors are anticipating the company's upcoming earnings report, with projections of a slight decline in EPS but a modest increase in revenue. Despite this, Copart holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and its valuation is in line with its industry average.

feed.title: Boston Properties (BXP) Q4 FFO and Revenues Lag Estimates
feed.summary: Boston Properties (BXP) reported Q4 FFO of $1.56 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.8 and down from $1.79 a year ago. The company's revenues for the quarter were $809.15 million, also falling short of the Zacks Consensus Estimate. Despite these misses, the company has surpassed revenue estimates three times in the last four quarters, and its stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), indicating expected in-line performance with the market.

feed.title: American Electric Power (AEP) Draws Focus as Barclays Refreshes Utilities Models
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) is gaining investor attention, with Barclays raising its price target to $121 and Ritholtz Wealth Management highlighting it as a strong utility. The company is engaged in a $72 billion grid modernization program, driven by increasing demand from electrification and data centers, and offers a dividend yield of approximately 3.26%. While acknowledging AEP's potential, the article suggests certain AI stocks may offer greater upside.

feed.title: Boeing takes $565m hit on KC-46 tanker
feed.summary: Boeing incurred a $565 million loss on its Air Force KC-46 tanker program in the last quarter of 2025, pushing total losses on the project to approximately $8 billion. This loss significantly impacted Boeing's defense and space business, leading to a $507 million loss for the quarter and a negative operating margin for the year. Despite the financial strain attributed to rising supply-chain costs and production support for the 767-based aircraft, Boeing executives expressed optimism for future contracts, planning to deliver more tankers in 2026 after renegotiating terms.

feed.title: Eaton (ETN) Surpasses Market Returns: Some Facts Worth Knowing
feed.summary: Eaton (ETN) closed at $341.19, up 2.68%, outperforming the S&P 500 but trailing its sector over the past month. The company is set to release earnings on February 3, 2026, with analysts forecasting significant EPS and revenue growth. Despite a slight downward revision in recent EPS projections, Eaton holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and is trading at a premium compared to its industry based on Forward P/E.

feed.title: Is It Time To Revisit Chipotle (CMG) After A 31% Share Price Decline?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Chipotle Mexican Grill's (CMG) valuation after a 31% share price decline over the past year. Using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis and Price/Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison, Simply Wall St suggests CMG may be overvalued, trading above its estimated intrinsic value and Fair Ratio. The article encourages investors to consider different valuation narratives and comprehensive analysis beyond single models.

feed.title: CocaCola (NYSE:KO) Trading 1.3% Higher - What's Next?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) shares rose 1.3% to $73.53 on Tuesday, with mid-day trading volume down 27% from the average. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" rating with a mean price target of $79.08, following several target increases from firms like Bank of America and UBS. Despite positive analyst sentiment, company insiders have been net sellers in the past 90 days, with executives Manuel Arroyo and Nancy Quan selling a significant number of shares.

feed.title: ADM to pay $40 million in US SEC settlement, avoids criminal charges By Reuters
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) will pay a $40 million civil penalty to the U.S. SEC for inflating the performance of a key business segment, avoiding criminal charges from the Department of Justice. The settlement concludes long-running investigations into accounting issues that led to financial report revisions and a former CFO's exit. ADM stated they are pleased to resolve the matter and have implemented extensive actions to enhance internal controls.

feed.title: ADM to pay $40 million in US SEC settlement, avoids criminal charges
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Co (ADM) has agreed to pay a $40 million civil penalty to the SEC to settle charges of inflating the performance of its "Nutrition" business segment, while avoiding criminal charges from the U.S. Department of Justice. The settlement concludes years of government investigations into accounting issues that forced ADM to revise financial reports and led to shareholder litigation. ADM cooperated with the SEC by conducting an internal investigation, reporting findings, and implementing new accounting controls.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) Trading 2.3% Higher - What's Next?
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin's stock (NYSE:LMT) saw a 2.3% increase in mid-day trading on Tuesday, reaching approximately $595, with trading volume 23% below average. The company's shares are currently trading above their 50- and 200-day moving averages. Analysts generally have a "Hold" rating on LMT, with a consensus target of $543.82, while institutional investors own about 74.2% of the stock.

feed.title: Humanoids’ Supply Chain Stall: Gartner’s Pilot Trap Prediction
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that fewer than 20 companies will scale humanoid robots to production in supply chains by 2028, largely due to technological immaturity and high costs. Despite ongoing pilot programs at companies like BMW, Mercedes, and Tesla, current humanoid robots struggle with versatility, dexterity, and integration in complex industrial environments. Polyfunctional robots, which are typically wheeled or specialized for specific tasks, are currently a more effective and cost-efficient alternative for dynamic warehouse operations.

feed.title: Less than a year after CrossFirst sale, Maddox exits as CEO of Busey Bank
feed.summary: Mike Maddox has abruptly exited his roles as vice chairman of First Busey and CEO of Busey Bank, less than a year after the sale of CrossFirst. He will receive a $4.36 million severance package. This change in leadership was announced by First Busey.

feed.title: ACNB Bank EVP Brett Fulk Sells 1,486 Shares of ACNB Corp
feed.summary: Brett Fulk, Executive Vice President of ACNB Bank, sold 1,486 shares of ACNB Corp on January 26, 2026, reducing his total holdings to 1,047.5187 shares. ACNB Corp, a financial holding company, had its shares trading at $48.52 with a price-to-earnings ratio of 13.77, which is higher than the industry median. According to GuruFocus, the stock is considered fairly valued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.04.

feed.title: Datavault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT) Trading Down 3.9% - Here's Why
feed.summary: Datavault AI (NASDAQ:DVLT) experienced a 3.9% drop in its stock price, trading down to $0.7252 with significantly lower than average daily volume. Analysts hold a "Hold" consensus rating with a $4.00 price target, despite one analyst having a "Buy" and another a "Sell" rating. The company reported a Q3 EPS of -$0.33 and $2.9 million in revenue, while insiders have sold a substantial number of shares in the past 90 days.

feed.title: Guild Howard sells SLB shares worth $659k
feed.summary: SLB LIMITED/NV's Chief Accounting Officer, Howard Guild, sold 13,268 shares of the company's common stock for approximately $659,419 on January 26, 2026. This transaction occurred amidst strong stock momentum and positive analyst outlooks, with SLB trading near its 52-week high. Despite some share disposals related to performance units, Guild still directly owns 22,407 shares of the company, which boasts a solid financial health and a consistent dividend history.

feed.title: Packaging Corp. (PKG) Q4 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
feed.summary: Packaging Corp. (PKG) reported Q4 2025 revenue of $2.36 billion, a 10.1% year-over-year increase, but missed the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.42 billion. The company also reported an EPS of $2.32, which was below the consensus estimate of $2.41. The article details key segment sales and operating income, comparing them against analyst estimates and year-ago figures, noting the stock's recent outperformance against the S&P 500.

feed.title: John Deere breaks ground on $125M distribution center in northern Indiana
feed.summary: John Deere has broken ground on a $125 million, 1.2 million-square-foot distribution center in northern Indiana, near Lowell and Hebron. This project is expected to create 150 jobs, with the state offering $2.5 million in incentive-based credits. The facility aims to streamline distribution operations and enhance product support for John Deere's diverse customer base in agriculture and other sectors.

feed.title: Boston Properties (BXP) Q4 FFO and Revenues Lag Estimates
feed.summary: Boston Properties (BXP) reported fourth-quarter FFO of $1.56 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.8 per share, and revenues of $809.15 million, which also fell short of expectations. The company's shares have lost about 4% year-to-date, and its FFO outlook suggests it will perform in line with the market in the near future, holding a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: ZBH.SW Zimmer Biomet (SIX) spikes to CHF67.50 pre-market 28 Jan 2026: monitor liquidity
feed.summary: ZBH.SW Zimmer Biomet (SIX) experienced a significant pre-market volume spike on January 28, 2026, trading at CHF67.50 with 110 shares against an average of 3.00, indicating high early interest. The stock is down 4.26% from its previous close, sitting at a year low. This event is highlighted as a short-term liquidity situation to watch leading up to upcoming earnings on February 10, 2026, with Meyka AI recommending a "HOLD" rating despite both upside and downside projections.

feed.title: Ann Arbor organization beginning petition drive for plan to form publicly-owned electric utility
feed.summary: A grassroots organization in Ann Arbor, "Ann Arbor for Public Power," is launching a petition drive to create a publicly-owned electric utility to replace DTE. Citing concerns over DTE's reliability and costs, the group plans to collect signatures for a ballot initiative that would establish a public utility governing board. DTE has warned that such a takeover could incur billions in new city debt without guaranteeing improved service or affordability.

feed.title: Advanced Biomed Signs Supplemental Agreement With Wei Ha Hui
feed.summary: Advanced Biomed (ADVB) has entered into a Supplemental Agreement with Wei Ha Hui concerning the spin-off of Advanced Biomed (HK). The agreement formalizes the repayment of $6,925,549 that Advanced Biomed (HK) owes the company, with the full amount to be repaid within one year. This amendment, approved by the board, adds the repayment obligation to the existing spin-off terms to ensure an orderly separation and recovery of intercompany receivables.

feed.title: Invesco Earnings Call Highlights Strategy Over Numbers
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd.'s recent Q4 earnings call emphasized strategic milestones and a client-centric transformation, signaling confidence in "accelerated profitable growth" for fiscal 2025. While the call was largely positive and forward-looking, it notably lacked specific quantitative financial details such as revenue, net income, or assets under management. The company directed investors to its website for more detailed financial information, leaving market participants with a qualitative but optimistic outlook on its future performance.

feed.title: Leidos (LDOS) Stock Sinks As Market Gains: Here's Why
feed.summary: Leidos (LDOS) stock experienced a 1.24% decline, closing at $187.15, while the broader S&P 500 gained 0.41%. Despite this daily dip, the company's shares have risen 2.16% over the past month, outperforming its sector and the S&P 500. Investors are anticipating Leidos's Q4 2025 earnings release on February 17, 2026, with analysts predicting an 8.44% EPS growth and a 2.58% revenue decrease year-over-year.

feed.title: ADM to pay $40M to settle SEC accounting fraud probe
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Co. (ADM) has agreed to pay $40 million to settle SEC allegations that former executives manipulated the accounting of a struggling nutrition business unit to meet company targets. The company did not admit or deny the allegations, and the DOJ closed its investigation without further action. However, the SEC also filed a lawsuit against former CFO Vikram Luthar, accusing him of coordinating profit shifts to prop up the nutrition segment.

feed.title: PLDT plans P1.22- billion subscription to Kayana shares
feed.summary: PLDT Inc. announced its board has approved a P1.22-billion subscription to additional shares in Kayana Solutions, Inc., maintaining its 45% stake. This investment aims to create new value and growth opportunities, leveraging Kayana Solutions' role as a data-powered digital experience company. PLDT previously invested in Kayana in September last year, and its shares saw a slight increase on Tuesday.

feed.title: Runway Growth Finance Launches Unsecured Notes Offering
feed.summary: Runway Growth Finance (RWAY) has initiated an underwritten offering of unsecured notes to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. The net proceeds will primarily be used to repay existing debt, including $51.75 million of 8.00% notes due in December 2027, and to support the prior acquisition of SWK Holdings, which will boost its healthcare investment portfolio to 31%. Despite a 14% dividend yield and a 30% discount to NAV, analysts maintain a cautious outlook on RWAY's future performance, with an average 1-year price target of $10.57.

feed.title: CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP) Insider Purchases $49,703.68 in Stock
feed.summary: CRISPR Therapeutics insider Naimish Patel recently acquired 1,508 shares of the company's stock, totaling $49,703.68. The transaction increases Patel's ownership to 1,508 shares. The company, which has a market cap of $5.17 billion, reported quarterly EPS of ($1.17) and revenue of $0.89 million, and it holds a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average target price of $68.15.

feed.title: ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF Options Spot-On: On January 27th, 192.17K Contracts Were Traded, With 1.24 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 27th, the ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF (TQQQ.US) saw significant options activity with 192.17K contracts traded, where call options accounted for 59.74% and put options for 40.26%. Open interest totaled approximately 1.24 million contracts, representing 78.72% of the average over the past 30 trading days. Notably, a put option with a $53.50 strike price expiring on June 18th, 2026, generated a turnover of $1.31 million.
